goal: 拆分 seal 写入 schema 与 legacy summary 读取模型

summary.title: 拆分 seal 写入 schema 与 legacy summary 读取模型
summary.what: 新增 SealSummaryInput 作为 seal --submit 的写入 DTO，保留 IntentSummary 作为 sealed event 的读取/落盘模型；seal starter/schema 不再暴露 legacy risks/followups/anti_patterns，submit 会在 raw JSON 层拒绝旧 summary signal key，并让 --allow-structured-signals 保持可解析但报迁移错误。同步 agents 模板 v27、本仓 skill、Copilot stub 与协议文档，明确候选 signal 只能落到 explicit command、review_notes 或最终回复。
summary.why: 旧 IntentSummary 同时承担历史读取模型和新 seal 输入模型，导致 legacy signal 字段继续泄漏给 agent，只能靠提示词反复禁止。拆分写入 DTO 可以在类型和 prepare schema 层消除误导，同时不迁移 actor-log schema，保证旧 ledger 仍可读。
summary.user_goal: 拆分 seal 写入 schema 与 legacy summary 读取模型

decisions.point: seal 写入模型与历史读取模型
decisions.chose: 新增 SealSummaryInput 作为 submit 输入，IntentSummary 继续作为 read/materialized/persisted summary。
decisions.rationale: 问题发生在 submit 边界；actor-log 历史需要继续解析旧字段，不能为清理 agent 输入而迁移账本 schema。

decisions.point: legacy signal key 的兼容策略
decisions.chose: seal --submit 在 raw JSON 层检测 summary.risks、summary.followups、summary.anti_patterns，只要出现就硬拒绝并提示重新 prepare。
decisions.rationale: 静默忽略会让旧 agent 误以为写入成功，自动转换 review_notes 会模糊 durable signal 语义；硬拒绝最能保护 ledger。

decisions.chose: 不新增 candidate_signals 实体；满足条件的 durable signal 走 explicit commands，不满足则放 review_notes 或最终回复。
decisions.rationale: 避免重新引入半 durable 队列；本次目标是切断旧字段泄漏，不是扩大数据模型。

decisions.chose: 通过 mainline agents update 只刷新 managed block 到 v27，并记录它与 int_9b78be92 的 docs-only AGENTS 指导审计相容。
decisions.rationale: 旧 proposed intent 的决策是完整 Mainline workflow 归 skill、AGENTS 保持轻指针；本次只更新 managed pointer/checksum，不改变该边界。

rejected.alternative: 删除 IntentSummary 里的 legacy risks/followups/anti_patterns 字段。
rejected.reason: 这会破坏旧 actor-log/read path 的兼容性，超出本次 submit 输入边界修复。

rejected.alternative: 保留 --allow-structured-signals 作为 escape hatch。
rejected.reason: 这会继续暗示 seal 仍可创建 durable signals，和 explicit command 模型冲突。
当前 review_notes、最终回复和 explicit commands 已能覆盖候选项，不需要新增实体。

review_notes: Smoke checks passed: seal --prepare starter/schema have no legacy signal keys; seal --submit rejects summary.risks; --allow-structured-signals reports a deprecated migration error; git diff --check passed; go run . agents check --json reports AGENTS.md in_sync at v27.

A broad go test ./internal/domain ./internal/core ./internal/engine ./internal/cli run and a property aggregate including TestPropertySealSubmitNeverMutatesOnValidationFailure were stopped after long no-output waits; focused affected tests above were used instead.

architectural_claims: Seal submit input is now a write DTO that cannot express legacy durable signal creation fields.
IntentSummary remains the read/materialized and actor-log event summary shape for historical compatibility.
Durable signal creation is only represented by explicit actor-log events from guard/risks/followups commands, not by seal summary prose.
behavioral_changes: seal_result_starter.summary and seal_result_schema.summary omit risks, followups, and anti_patterns keys.
mainline seal --submit rejects payloads that contain summary.risks, summary.followups, or summary.anti_patterns even when empty.
mainline seal --submit --allow-structured-signals is parseable but exits with a deprecation/migration error.
Seal lint converts SealSummaryInput to IntentSummary for existing decision/fingerprint checks while legacy signal lint remains only for read/materialized summaries.
Agent guidance now tells agents to propose insufficiently promoted candidate signals in the final response rather than inventing durable signal fields.
security_implications: No runtime security behavior changed; the change reduces the chance that agent-authored seal prose becomes mistaken for durable shared action signals.
migration_notes: No actor-log migration is required; old IntentSummary legacy fields remain readable.
Old temporary .ml-cache/seal.json files containing summary.risks, summary.followups, or summary.anti_patterns must be regenerated with mainline seal --prepare --json.
Agents or scripts that relied on seal-embedded action signal fields must use mainline risks add, mainline followups add, or human-confirmed mainline guard add.
